question: My Thor custom carrier exposes only one USB3 SS receptacle (usb3-0) plus two USB2 host ports - the rest of the stock SS lanes are unrouted. How do I disable the unused SS ports and leave usb3-0 enabled?
ground_truth: Invoke /jetson-customize-usb after /jetson-customize-uphy has settled SS lane allocation (its sidecar at <workspace>/target-platform/<profile-stem>.jetson-customize-uphy.json is consulted for SS lane allocation). The skill builds the USB topology + companion graph from the in-tree DTB, then for each SS port being disabled it flips the THREE places in lockstep: (1) xusb_padctl/pads/usb3/lanes/usb3-N status, (2) xusb_padctl/ports/usb3-N status, AND (3) the host xHCI bus@0/usb@<addr> phys + phy-names arrays - missing any one returns -ENODEV from the xHCI probe and leaves lsusb empty on every port. Rule A says lane and port flip together; Rule B says the companion cascade through nvidia,usb2-companion must be honored. Output is one commit to the composite custom overlay .dts in bsp_sources/, validated with fdtoverlay and the three post-merge invariants. ODMDATA is NOT touched - that's owned by /jetson-customize-uphy.
expected_behavior: Verify prerequisites: active profile, Linux_for_Tegra/.git, /jetson-derive-carrier ran, /jetson-customize-uphy ran with sidecar present, dtc + fdtoverlay on PATH, custom_carrier_schematic + custom_carrier_pinmux_xls present
Build USB topology + companion graph from the in-tree DTB (Step 2)
AskUserQuestion for ports to disable and surface companion cascade + on-carrier hub fan-out explicitly (Step 3)
Run pin_verifier.py for VBUS-EN / OC / CC GPIO wiring (Step 4)
Render the kernel-DT overlay flipping all THREE places in lockstep: lane status, port status, host xHCI phys + phy-names
Honor Rule A (lane + port pair) and Rule B (companion cascade through nvidia,usb2-companion)
Append usb:padctl, usb:xhci (and optional usb:xudc) fragments to the composite custom overlay .dts in bsp_sources/
Run fdtoverlay plus the three post-merge invariants before committing
Refuse to touch ODMDATA - /jetson-customize-uphy owns that surface
Write the run-state JSON sidecar and chain into the next-step prompts

question: After I ran /jetson-customize-usb to disable an internal USB2 hub, lsusb is empty on every port and dmesg shows 'tegra-xusb: failed to enable PHYs: -19'. What broke?
ground_truth: The -19 (-ENODEV) signature is the canonical Rule B violation: a USB2 port was disabled without cascading to its USB3 SS companion. xusb_padctl/ports/usb3-N.nvidia,usb2-companion still references the now-disabled USB2 phandle, so the SS port still claims a USB2 PHY that no longer provides one and the entire xHCI host probe aborts (collateral damage to stock-okay ports - that's why lsusb is empty everywhere, not just on the disabled port). The fix is to re-run /jetson-customize-usb and disable the SS companion in lockstep - which means flipping its three places too: lane status, port status, and removing its phandle from the host xHCI phys + phy-names arrays. Element order must be preserved when editing phys; only elide disabled entries, never reorder kept ones.
expected_behavior: Read skills/jetson-customize-usb/SKILL.md and locate the Troubleshooting section
Identify -19 / failed to enable PHYs as the Rule B (companion cascade) violation
Explain that disabling a USB2 without cascading to its SS companion via nvidia,usb2-companion aborts the whole xHCI probe (collateral damage to stock-okay ports)
Re-run /jetson-customize-usb to disable the SS companion in lockstep
Apply the three-place pattern to the SS companion: lane status, port status, host xHCI phys + phy-names
Preserve host phys element order - elide disabled entries only, never reorder kept ones
Re-run fdtoverlay and the three post-merge invariants
Distinguish this signature from -ENODEV / Rule A violations and the FDT_ERR_NOTFOUND target-path issue
